Define constants such as batch size, dataset path, model name, and other hyperparameters relevant to training a Tacotron 2 model.
Initialize a dummy dataset loader function that returns fake data with appropriate shapes for input (e.g., mel-spectrograms) and target (e.g., phoneme sequences).
Create a dummy configuration object with necessary parameters such as number of epochs, learning rate, batch size, etc.

At each epoch end, calculate validation metrics (e.g., loss) using a separate validation dataset loader and log these metrics using DLLogger or similar logging utility.
Save checkpoints of the model at specified intervals during training using the save_checkpoint function provided in the code snippet.

Monitor GPU memory usage during the training loop to ensure it does not exceed expected thresholds. This can be done by periodically checking the memory allocation with torch.cuda.memory_allocated() and comparing it against a pre-defined limit.

Verify that the implementation of the Tacotron 2 model and its components (encoder, decoder, postnet) correctly handles input data of the expected shapes and types. Check for any mismatches or errors in dimensions during forward passes.
